chore: commit concurrent agent work in progress

Co-Authored-By: Claude <noreply@anthropic.com>

files.patch:
@@ -144,7 +144,7 @@
 {"id":"bd-qlt.8","title":"Implement CorrectionEvidenceLedger with per-window correction tracking","description":"PHASE 3 ADAPTIVE INTELLIGENCE -- Persistent evidence ledger that records every speculation decision for analysis, debugging, and adaptive improvement.

FILE: src/speculation.rs (extend)

WHY:
The existing RoutingEvidenceLedger (backend/mod.rs) tracks backend selection decisions. The CorrectionEvidenceLedger is its speculation analog: it records every window's fast/quality comparison, correction decision, and drift metrics. This serves three purposes:
1. Post-hoc analysis: "Why did the pipeline correct window 47?"
2. Adaptive control: The SpeculationWindowController reads this to tune window size
3. Debugging: Full replay of every decision for deterministic reproduction

RELATIONSHIP TO EXISTING EVIDENCE INFRASTRUCTURE:
- RoutingEvidenceLedger (backend/mod.rs:229-381) -- tracks backend selection, capacity 200
- PipelineCx::record_evidence() (orchestrator.rs) -- accepts Value entries
- EvidenceLedgerBuilder (accelerate.rs) -- builds acceleration evidence
- This new CorrectionEvidenceLedger follows the same pattern: structured entries, bounded capacity, JSON export

TYPES:

pub struct CorrectionEvidenceLedger {
    entries: VecDeque<CorrectionEvidenceEntry>,
    capacity: usize,
    total_recorded: u64,
}

pub struct CorrectionEvidenceEntry {
    pub entry_id: u64,
    pub window_id: u64,
    pub run_id: String,
    pub timestamp_rfc3339: String,
    
    // Fast model info
    pub fast_model_id: String,
    pub fast_latency_ms: u64,
    pub fast_confidence_mean: f64,
    pub fast_segment_count: usize,
    
    // Quality model info
    pub quality_model_id: String,
    pub quality_latency_ms: u64,
    pub quality_confidence_mean: f64,
    pub quality_segment_count: usize,
    
    // Drift metrics
    pub drift: CorrectionDrift,
    
    // Decision
    pub decision: String,          // "confirmed" or "corrected"
    pub tolerance_snapshot: CorrectionTolerance,
    
    // Controller state at decision time
    pub window_size_ms: u64,
    pub correction_rate_at_decision: f64,
    pub controller_action: String, // "shrink", "hold", "grow"
    pub controller_confidence: f64,
    pub fallback_active: bool,
    pub fallback_reason: Option<String>,
}

impl CorrectionEvidenceLedger {
    pub fn new(capacity: usize) -> Self
    pub fn record(&mut self, entry: CorrectionEvidenceEntry)
    pub fn total_recorded(&self) -> u64
    pub fn entries(&self) -> &VecDeque<CorrectionEvidenceEntry>
    pub fn to_evidence_json(&self) -> Value  // For PipelineCx::record_evidence()
    pub fn diagnostics(&self) -> Value       // Summary stats as JSON
    
    // Analysis methods
    pub fn correction_rate(&self) -> f64
    pub fn mean_fast_latency(&self) -> f64
    pub fn mean_quality_latency(&self) -> f64
    pub fn mean_wer(&self) -> f64
    pub fn latency_savings_pct(&self) -> f64  // How much faster user sees results vs quality-only
    
    // Window size trend
    pub fn window_size_trend(&self) -> Vec<(u64, u64)>  // (window_id, window_size_ms) pairs
}

INTEGRATION WITH PipelineCx:
At the end of a speculative run, the pipeline calls:
  pcx.record_evidence(&correction_ledger.to_evidence_json());
This makes the correction evidence part of the run's permanent evidence trail, alongside routing evidence and acceleration evidence.

INTEGRATION WITH STORAGE:
The evidence entries should be persisted in the RunReport.evidence array, which flows through to frankensqlite via the existing storage pipeline. No new storage schema needed.

CAPACITY & BOUNDS:
- Default capacity: 500 entries (more than routing ledger's 200, because windows are smaller units)
- Old entries are evicted FIFO when capacity is reached
- total_recorded tracks the true count even after eviction

DEPENDS ON: scs.5 (CorrectionTracker for decisions), scs.7 (controller state for evidence entries)","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-22T22:20:27.020961507Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-22T23:19:08.507915211Z","closed_at":"2026-02-22T23:19:08.507893440Z","close_reason":"done","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["adaptive","evidence","phase-3","telemetry"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-qlt.8","depends_on_id":"bd-qlt","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-25T20:17:53Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-qlt.8","depends_on_id":"bd-qlt.5","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-25T20:17:53Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-qlt.8","depends_on_id":"bd-qlt.7","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-25T20:17:53Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}
 {"id":"bd-qlt.9","title":"Add TUI correction rendering with visual retraction markers","description":"PHASE 4 SURFACE INTEGRATION -- Makes corrections visible in the TUI.

FILE: src/tui.rs (extend existing, behind #[cfg(feature = "tui")])

WHY:
When the quality model corrects a fast model result, the TUI must show the user what happened. Without visual correction rendering, the user sees text appear, then silently change -- which is confusing and feels like a bug. With proper rendering, corrections are a feature: the user sees instant results with clear visual markers when corrections arrive.

CURRENT TUI STATE:
- LiveTranscriptionView (tui.rs:534-704): push_segment, scroll, auto_scroll
- SpeakerColorMap: ANSI color-coded speaker labels
- render_speaker_colored_line(): segment -> colored string
- SearchState, TranscriptFilter, TranscriptSearch: search/filter infrastructure

WHAT TO ADD:

1. CorrectionState tracking per-segment:
   pub(crate) enum SegmentCorrectionState {
       Original,       // Not part of any speculation (file mode, no --speculative)
       Speculative,    // Fast model result, pending quality confirmation
       Confirmed,      // Quality model agreed
       Retracted,      // Quality model disagreed, this segment is no longer valid
       Corrected,      // This segment is the quality model's replacement
   }

2. Extend LiveTranscriptionView:
   - Add correction_states: HashMap<usize, SegmentCorrectionState> (keyed by segment index)
   - Add push_speculative_segment(&mut self, segment, seq) -- marks as Speculative
   - Add retract_segment(&mut self, seq) -- finds segment by seq, marks Retracted
   - Add push_correction(&mut self, replaces_seq, corrected_segments) -- marks old as Retracted, new as Corrected
   - Add correction_count(&self) -> usize
   - Add speculation_stats_line(&self) -> String -- "Corrections: 3/47 windows (6.4%)"

3. Visual rendering rules:
   - Speculative segments: render with a subtle "~" prefix (e.g., "~ hello world")
   - Confirmed segments: render normally (remove "~" prefix)
   - Retracted segments: render with strikethrough ANSI codes (\x1b[9m...\x1b[0m) and dim color
   - Corrected segments: render with a brief highlight (bold for 2 ticks, then normal)
   - The correction transition should be visible but not jarring

4. Status bar extension:
   - Add speculation mode indicator: "[SPEC]" when speculative streaming is active
   - Show correction rate: "Corrections: 3/47 (6.4%)"
   - Show current window size: "Window: 3000ms"

5. Correction history panel (optional, toggled with 'c' key):
   - Shows last N corrections as a scrollable list
   - Each entry: "Window #47: 'hello wrold' -> 'hello world' (WER: 0.08)"
   - Useful for debugging and understanding pipeline behavior

DESIGN CHOICE -- WHY NOT JUST REPLACE TEXT SILENTLY:
- Users notice silent changes and find them unsettling
- Showing corrections builds trust ("the system is self-improving")
- The correction history is valuable for understanding model behavior
- Power users want to see correction rates to tune their model selection

BACKWARD COMPATIBILITY:
- When --speculative is not active, all segments are SegmentCorrectionState::Original
- No visual changes to non-speculative mode
- All existing TUI tests must continue to pass

DEPENDS ON: scs.5 (CorrectionTracker types for SegmentCorrectionState mapping)","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-22T22:20:55.079345568Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-22T23:13:30.244415940Z","closed_at":"2026-02-22T23:13:30.244393508Z","close_reason":"done","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["phase-4","rendering","streaming","tui"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-qlt.9","depends_on_id":"bd-qlt","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-25T20:17:53Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-qlt.9","depends_on_id":"bd-qlt.5","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-25T20:17:53Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}
 {"id":"bd-qur","title":"rch: stabilize remote quality-gate execution for franken_whisper","description":"Remote quality gates are nondeterministic due to worker state drift and fail-open behavior: (1) some workers compile with stale /data/projects/asupersync causing E0308 in three_lane.rs; (2) some workers fail cargo test with no-space errors; (3) remote failures can trigger local fallback, violating offload policy. Investigate deterministic remote-only gate strategy for this repo and document/implement minimal local controls to keep cargo fmt/check/clippy/test reproducible via rch.","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","assignee":"ubuntu","created_at":"2026-02-25T08:35:11.545757424Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-25T08:53:09.834196150Z","closed_at":"2026-02-25T08:53:09.834173227Z","close_reason":"Added rch quality-gate wrapper with remote-only enforcement, retry logic, worker block/restore control, and README runbook; validated fmt/check/clippy remote pass under controlled routing","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"comments":[{"id":14,"issue_id":"bd-qur","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"Implemented repo-local remote quality-gate wrapper and documentation.

Changes:
- Added scripts/run_quality_gates_rch.sh:
  - Runs mandatory gates via rch (`fmt`, `check`, `clippy`, optional `test`).
  - Enforces remote-only semantics by failing on `[RCH] local` fallback.
  - Retries known transient/worker errors on fresh remote target dirs.
  - Added routing controls:
    - `BLOCK_WORKERS` (comma-separated) to temporarily disable problematic workers during a run.
    - Auto-restores disabled workers on script exit via trap.
  - Backward compatibility: `DRAIN_WORKERS` still accepted as alias input for `BLOCK_WORKERS`.
- Updated README Testing section with usage and env toggles:
  - `scripts/run_quality_gates_rch.sh`
  - `RUN_TEST_GATE=0`
  - `MAX_ATTEMPTS`
  - `BLOCK_WORKERS=vmi1149989`

Validation:
- Script syntax: `bash -n scripts/run_quality_gates_rch.sh` => pass.
- Lifecycle smoke: `MAX_ATTEMPTS=0 RUN_TEST_GATE=0 BLOCK_WORKERS=vmi1149989 scripts/run_quality_gates_rch.sh || true`
  - worker disable + auto-restore verified.
- Practical run: `MAX_ATTEMPTS=1 RUN_TEST_GATE=0 BLOCK_WORKERS=vmi1149989 scripts/run_quality_gates_rch.sh`
  - pass: fmt/check/clippy all succeeded remotely.
  - scheduler did not route to blocked worker.

Residual risk:
- Full `cargo test` remains the longest/highest-pressure gate and can still fail on worker storage/toolchain instability; wrapper now provides deterministic controls and explicit fail surfaces rather than silent/local fallback.
","created_at":"2026-02-25T08:53:06Z"}]}
-{"id":"bd-rjc","title":"U2 core follow-up: root-cause concurrent write-path sum drift/corruption","description":"While executing bd-2im with rch-offloaded runs, ci_scale remains unstable despite runtime-containment and retry hardening in harness. Evidence on 2026-02-25: (1) default mix run failed with final_sum 1024208 vs expected_sum 1024211; (2) transfer-only diagnostic failed with sum_delta=0 but final_sum 1024016 vs expected 1024000; (3) deposit-only diagnostic failed with final_sum 1024911 vs expected 1024943; (4) optional rusqlite verifier read hit malformed schema/rootpage on one run, indicating potential on-disk structural inconsistency under concurrent writer stress. Scope: isolate and fix root cause in fsqlite-core/pager/mvcc commit path (lost update/partial apply/corruption), add deterministic reproducer, then restore strict ci_scale invariant closure.","notes":"2026-02-25 investigation: ci_scale drift reproduces after retained harness hardening (rollback-required + reconnect). Transfer-only control and deposit-only control both drifted, indicating issue is not limited to transfer semantics. Disjoint-worker account-range containment did not remove drift and was reverted. rusqlite-verifier diagnostic observed malformed schema/rootpage under stress in one run; diagnostic code reverted to avoid destabilizing suite, but evidence retained in bead text. Remaining work: isolate deterministic minimal reproducer in fsqlite-core/pager commit path.","status":"in_progress","priority":0,"issue_type":"bug","assignee":"OliveBison","created_at":"2026-02-25T22:10:57.920906200Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-26T03:19:42.721630706Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["cross-repo","frankensqlite","mvcc","quality"],"comments":[{"id":19,"issue_id":"bd-rjc","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"Support slice (EmeraldSparrow): added deterministic hot-row sum/integrity repro in /data/projects/frankensqlite at crates/fsqlite-e2e/tests/bd_rjc_deterministic_hot_row_sum_probe.rs. Validation: rch exec -- cargo test -p fsqlite-e2e --test bd_rjc_deterministic_hot_row_sum_probe -- --nocapture (pass: 1 passed, 0 failed).","created_at":"2026-02-26T02:58:44Z"},{"id":20,"issue_id":"bd-rjc","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"Support slice (EmeraldSparrow): hardened MVCC finalize path in /data/projects/frankensqlite/crates/fsqlite-mvcc/src/begin_concurrent.rs to preserve commit-index update + lock release when prepared session is missing/inactive during finalize. Added regression: test_finalize_releases_locks_and_updates_commit_index_when_session_missing. rch validation passed for new mvcc unit test and bd_rjc_deterministic_hot_row_sum_probe e2e test.","created_at":"2026-02-26T03:09:59Z"},{"id":21,"issue_id":"bd-rjc","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"Validation update (EmeraldSparrow, offloaded with rch): cargo check -p fsqlite-mvcc -p fsqlite-e2e passed. Clippy on touched crates surfaced unrelated pre-existing warnings/errors outside my edits: crates/fsqlite-vfs/src/unix.rs (useless_conversion) and crates/fsqlite-parser/src/semantic.rs (collapsible_match).","created_at":"2026-02-26T03:19:42Z"}]}
